task: I’m trying to put together a Christmas gift built around Pokémon cards, but I want a realistic backup plan in case the main item is sold out. Please start on Collector Store and look up the Pokémon Phantasmal Flames Booster Elite Trainer Box, then open the actual product page and check whether it says it’s in stock or sold out, and note the listed price so I know if the original idea is still viable. After that, go to Best Buy and search for Pokémon trading cards or Pokémon card gift items and pick one gift option that looks like a reasonable substitute, making sure to open the product page so I can see the listing myself and leaving that tab open as a reference. Then use that same general idea on Walgreens by searching for Pokémon trading cards and finding two available options with prices, opening each Walgreens product in its own tab so I can compare them side by side and verify they’re actually live listings. In the end, give me a short backup-plan summary with the Collector Store stock status and price, the Best Buy product name and price, and the two Walgreens options with their prices.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Collector Store, searched for the "Pokémon Phantasmal Flames Booster Elite Trainer Box," and accessed the product page. In step 7, the agent explicitly identified the stock status ("Low stock: 6 left") and the price ($144.99). This information is confirmed by the agent's summary in step 27. The requirement to find the page and capture the stock status and price is satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to Best Buy, searched for "Pokémon trading cards," and opened a product page for the "Pokémon Trading Card Game: Scarlet & Violet Prismatic Evolutions Super-Premium Collection." The action history (Step 10) confirms the page was opened and the title includes the product name. The agent also correctly identified the price ($89.99) in its summary (Step 27).

R3: failure - The agent was tasked with finding two available Pokémon trading card options on Walgreens and recording their names and prices from live product pages. While the agent successfully opened two Walgreens product pages, both pages were for Pokémon-themed toys (a "Battle Figure" and a "Nanoblock Pikachu"), not Pokémon trading cards. The agent explicitly acknowledged in its final summary that it could not find purchasable Pokémon trading card listings at Walgreens and instead provided these toy alternatives. Therefore, the requirement to find two Pokémon *trading card* options was not met.

R4: success - The agent provided a summary that included the Collector Store status and price ($144.99, low stock), the Best Buy backup option and price ($89.99), and two Walgreens options with prices ($9.99 and $12.99). While the agent correctly noted that the Walgreens options were "gift-adjacent" rather than actual trading cards (due to availability issues), it fulfilled the requirement to provide a summary that acts as a practical backup plan based on the findings from the navigation. All requested components are present and accurate to the agent's findings.

rubric_results.requirement: Find the Collector Store product page for the Pokémon Phantasmal Flames Booster Elite Trainer Box and capture its stock status and listed price.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the browser is on the Collector Store product page showing the product title plus a visible in-stock or sold-out indicator and a price.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to the Collector Store, searched for the "Pokémon Phantasmal Flames Booster Elite Trainer Box," and accessed the product page. In step 7, the agent explicitly identified the stock status ("Low stock: 6 left") and the price ($144.99). This information is confirmed by the agent's summary in step 27. The requirement to find the page and capture the stock status and price is satisfied.

rubric_results.requirement: Identify one Pokémon card gift option on Best Buy and record its product name and price from the product page.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm a Best Buy product page is open with a Pokémon card-related item, and the visible page shows the product name and price.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully navigated to Best Buy, searched for "Pokémon trading cards," and opened a product page for the "Pokémon Trading Card Game: Scarlet & Violet Prismatic Evolutions Super-Premium Collection." The action history (Step 10) confirms the page was opened and the title includes the product name. The agent also correctly identified the price ($89.99) in its summary (Step 27).

rubric_results.requirement: Find two available Pokémon trading card options on Walgreens and record the product name and price for each from live product pages.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm two Walgreens product tabs are open, each showing a Pokémon trading card listing with visible product names and prices.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent was tasked with finding two available Pokémon trading card options on Walgreens and recording their names and prices from live product pages. While the agent successfully opened two Walgreens product pages, both pages were for Pokémon-themed toys (a "Battle Figure" and a "Nanoblock Pikachu"), not Pokémon trading cards. The agent explicitly acknowledged in its final summary that it could not find purchasable Pokémon trading card listings at Walgreens and instead provided these toy alternatives. Therefore, the requirement to find two Pokémon *trading card* options was not met.

rubric_results.requirement: Provide a short summary that combines the Collector Store stock status and price, the Best Buy backup option with price, and the two Walgreens options with prices as a practical gift backup plan.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can compare the final written summary against the visible product pages and confirm all requested items and prices are included accurately.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a summary that included the Collector Store status and price ($144.99, low stock), the Best Buy backup option and price ($89.99), and two Walgreens options with prices ($9.99 and $12.99). While the agent correctly noted that the Walgreens options were "gift-adjacent" rather than actual trading cards (due to availability issues), it fulfilled the requirement to provide a summary that acts as a practical backup plan based on the findings from the navigation. All requested components are present and accurate to the agent's findings.
